findings.issue: French text rendered on the EN site: the forecast method disclaimer in the beach sheet displays raw French from the data pipeline â€” 'Persistance + bancs satellite + vent. Fiabilite decroit apres J+3.' It sits directly under the premium forecast teaser ('Next days: Tmrw Fri Sat â€” Unlock'), i.e. at the exact point of conversion.
findings.evidence: Mobile sheet text dump line 157 (scripts/tmp-audit-fl/mobile-2-beach-sheet.txt). Source: scripts/lib/forecast.cjs:399-412 â€” all 5 forecastDisclaimer variants are French-only and get baked into public/api/copernicus/florida/sargassum.json served to EN/ES sites. Rendered raw by Sargasses_PROD.jsx:1989-1991 ({weeklyData.forecastDisclaimer}). The app-side interpolation fallbacks are also FR-only (Sargasses_PROD.jsx:612, 619-621: 'Banc detecte pres des plages voisines â€” risque de derive.', 'DÃ©rive possible vers la cÃ´te').
findings.suggestion: Make forecastDisclaimer/driftLabel locale-aware: emit a method key (e.g. forecastMethod already exists: 'banks-persistence') in the JSON and translate it client-side via the existing _t(lang, fr, en, es) helper, instead of shipping prose in the data. Fixes all 3 EN/ES domains at once without touching pipeline thresholds.

findings.page: beach SEO pages + hub pages (/beaches/miami-beach/, /sargassum-season-florida/, etc.)
findings.issue: All 18+ indexed static URLs serve their content inside <noscript> only; a real visitor from Google gets the generic statewide map â€” the promised content never appears. Landing on /beaches/miami-beach/ ('Miami Beach Sargassum Today â€” Live Status') does NOT auto-open the Miami Beach sheet, even 9s after load. The SERP promise is broken and the visitor must re-find their beach manually; same for the season guide and forecast pages whose rich editorial content is crawler-only.
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/mobile-4-deeplink-miamibeach.png (generic map, no sheet, 9s after domcontentloaded, final URL unchanged). Body of /beaches/miami-beach/ is <noscript><article>â€¦</article></noscript> + <div id=root> (scripts/tmp-audit-fl/beaches_miami-beach_.html). Content quality itself is excellent native English.
findings.suggestion: On SPA boot, parse location.pathname: /beaches/<slug>/ â†’ auto-open that beach's sheet (and center map); hub paths (/sargassum-season-florida/ â€¦) â†’ open the Beaches list or scrollable content panel matching the page intent. This converts SEO landings into the decision moment instead of a cold start, and reduces soft-404/content-parity risk.

findings.issue: The paywall's proof line contradicts the map the user just saw: modal says '4/12 beaches clean right now Â· satellite 24/7' while the header banner behind it says 'BEACHES Â· 12 CLEAN' and every pin is green/Clean. Two different definitions of 'clean' (Beach Score â‰¥70 vs sargassum status) shown simultaneously at the moment of payment â€” looks like the product can't agree with itself.
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/mobile-3-paywall.txt: '4/12 beaches clean right now' vs body 'BEACHES Â· 12 CLEAN'. Code: Sargasses_PROD.jsx:3675 _cleanCount=_islandLvls.filter(b=>b.score>=70).length (score-based) vs the status-based count in the header.
findings.suggestion: Use the sargassum-status clean count in the paywall line (same as header), or reword to what it actually measures: '4/12 beaches scoring 70+ today'. One definition of 'clean' per screen.

findings.issue: Map basemap paints very late: at 8s after load on desktop the screen is a pure dark void with floating green dots (no land, no labels); on mobile at 6s large rectangular tile holes remain; full basemap only at ~9-15s. The map is the hero product â€” first impression is 'broken'. 25 of 33 tile requests were aborted (zoom/fitBounds thrash re-requesting tiles at intermediate zooms).
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/desktop-1-home.png (8s, zero tiles), mobile-1-home.png (6s, tile holes), mobile-home-15s.png (fully loaded), tiles-check output: 'tiles ok: 8 fail: 25, FAILED net::ERR_ABORTED https://a.basemaps.cartocdn.com/rastertiles/voyager/11/565/867.png' â€” tiles requested at z11 then aborted while initial view is z7.
findings.suggestion: Set the initial Leaflet view directly to the region's center/zoom from regions/florida.json before the tile layer mounts (avoid post-load fitBounds animation that aborts a full tile set), add a land-toned map background color matching the basemap so unloaded areas don't read as black, and consider keepBuffer/updateWhenZooming:false on the tile layer.

findings.page: Premium paywall modal (variant sample_first du test pw_cta_order)
findings.issue: Fabricated social proof on a brand-new market: the sample_first paywall variant shows '<N> people got their morning pick today' where N = max(7, cleanCount*3), with a hardcoded '127 people' fallback â€” invented numbers presented as real usage, on a site with ~0 subscribers. If a Floridian spots it (or an FTC-minded competitor), trust is gone.
findings.evidence: Sargasses_PROD.jsx:4207: {_cleanCount>0?`${Math.max(7,_cleanCount*3)} ${lang==="en"?"people":â€¦}`:(lang==="en"?"127 people":â€¦)} + 'got their morning pick today. Try 24h â€” no card.'
findings.suggestion: Replace with a true number (morning-brief emails actually sent across the network: 'X briefs sent across 5 regions this morning') or drop the line on regions below a real threshold. Never synthesize counts â€” this also violates the project's own 'pas de donnÃ©es inventÃ©es' principle.

findings.issue: The sheet's first screenful is mostly dead space: the beach photo renders as a thin ~90px sliver (showing only building tops/sky) followed by ~200px of blank white before the 'Clean' chip and the beach name; the score card and 'GO FOR IT' verdict land below the fold on mobile. Weak hierarchy at the most-visited surface of the app.
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/desktop-2b-sheet.png and mobile-2-beach-sheet.png â€” Palm Beach sheet: photo strip at top, large empty white block, name at ~55% of viewport height.
findings.suggestion: Give the hero photo a proper height (160-200px, object-fit:cover, gradient into the card) and pull status chip + name + score into the first 350px so the verdict ('GOOD 72 â€” GO FOR IT') is visible without scrolling. That verdict is the product.

findings.issue: Contradictory accuracy claims visible to the same user: the sheet's forecast block says 'Reliable up to 4 days. 46% confidence tomorrow.' immediately above the 'ðŸ”’ UNLOCK FORECAST' CTA, while /methodology/ advertises 'next-day status is correct 86% of the time'. 46% reads as a coin flip â€” it undercuts the exact thing being sold, and the two numbers side-by-side look inconsistent.
findings.evidence: mobile-2-beach-sheet.txt lines 149-151 ('Reliable up to 4 days. 46% confidence tomorrow.' / 'ðŸ”’ UNLOCK FORECAST') vs methodology_.html ('next-day status is correct 86% of the time (mean AFAI error 0.036)').
findings.suggestion: Show the published accuracy framing at the point of sale ('Next-day forecasts verified 86% accurate â€” see methodology') and keep the per-day confidence as a small reliability bar without a sub-50% headline number next to the paywall CTA. If 46% is the honest pixel-level confidence, express it as the day-4+ horizon caveat instead of 'tomorrow'.

findings.issue: Plan messaging mismatch inside the modal: step 1 defaults to Annual ('Start my daily pick â€” 7 days free' / 'Then $79/yr Â· cancel in 1 click'), but the embedded checkout step in the same modal says 'START YOUR FREE TRIAL â€” $0 today Â· then $9.99/mo in 7 days'. If the annual selection doesn't propagate to the checkout step copy, users see two different prices for the same commitment.
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/mobile-3-paywall.txt: 'Annual $79/yr -33% â€¦ Then $79/yr Â· cancel in 1 click' followed by 'â† Back / START YOUR FREE TRIAL / $0 today Â· then $9.99/mo in 7 days Â· cancel in 1 click'. (Captured from pre-rendered DOM â€” verify which copy shows when Annual is selected.)
findings.suggestion: Bind the checkout-step subtitle to the selected plan ('then $79/yr in 7 days' when Annual is selected). A price flip between click and card entry is a classic checkout-abandon trigger.

findings.issue: Forecast bars show bare percentages ('7% TODAY Â· 5% TMRW Â· 5% FRI') with no unit or label â€” a tourist can't tell if 7% is risk, coverage, or confidence. Meaningless numbers dilute an otherwise strong free teaser.
findings.evidence: mobile-2-beach-sheet.txt lines 139-148: '7% TODAY 5% 5% TMRW 5% FRI 5% SAT'.
findings.suggestion: Label the metric once ('sargassum risk') or map to the existing Clean/Moderate/Avoid vocabulary instead of raw percentages.

findings.issue: A hairline rectangle (region bbox border) is visible around the Florida map area on desktop â€” reads as an unfinished debug artifact on an otherwise clean app-feel layout (which correctly keeps the mobile-style centered dock/search, per the design charter).
findings.evidence: scripts/tmp-audit-fl/desktop-1b-home-12s.png â€” faint rectangle outline around the state.
findings.suggestion: Remove the stroke from the maxBounds/bbox rectangle layer (fill/stroke opacity 0) or clip it outside the viewport.

findings.issue: A push pre-prompt banner ('Get notified when your favorite beaches change. â€” Activate') slides over the header during an early session without a user-intent trigger (appeared while a beach sheet was open, overlapping the timeline pill).
findings.suggestion: Gate the push pre-prompt on an intent signal already in the app (favoriting a beach, submitting the email capture, or 2nd visit), per the project's own 'no popup without data' rule.

findings.issue: regions/florida.json declares secondaryLangs:['es'] and the app header has an ES toggle, but the domain exposes zero indexable Spanish pages and hreflang only lists en + x-default â€” for a Miami market where Spanish is the home language of ~half the metro, the ES experience is invisible to search.
findings.evidence: home.html head: only <link rel=alternate hreflang=en> + x-default; sitemap has no /es/ URLs; regions/florida.json secondaryLangs ['es'].
findings.suggestion: Generate the existing hub pages in Spanish under /es/ (the region-seo-pages.cjs ES translation table already exists for CancÃºn) with proper hreflang pairs â€” cheap reuse, large untapped audience.
